claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by asking you a few questions to understand your situation.

First, do you have any children? If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you have
- Their names (or you can use pseudonyms if you prefer)
- Their ages

This will help me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. Her name is Alex and she's 18 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask a few additional questions to determine eligibility:

1. Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they're in approved full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or Scottish Highers) or certain approved training programs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in approved education. She's studying for her A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to ask about your circumstances:

Do you currently live in the UK, or are you otherwise considered responsible for Alex under UK rules (for example, are you contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards her upbringing)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. Alex lives here with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

1. Are you the person primarily responsible for Alex? In other words, are you the main person who looks after her and she lives with you?

(If there's another person who might also be considered responsible for Alex, such as another parent or guardian living in the same household, please let me know as only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm the person primarily responsible for Alex. She lives with me and I look after her. There's no one else claiming for her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

1. Is Alex currently in hospital or being looked after by local authorities (in care)?

2. When did Alex turn 20 (or when will she turn 20)? Child Benefit for young people in education or training stops on 31 August after they turn 19, so I need to confirm she hasn't reached that limit yet.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
No, Alex isn't in hospital or in care - she's living at home with me.

As for her age, she's 18 now, so she won't turn 20 for another two years.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for clarifying. One final question:

When does Alex turn 19? (Or when did she turn 18 - what month/year?)

This is important because Child Benefit for young people in approved education or training stops on 31 August following their 19th birthday. I need to confirm whether Alex is still within the eligible age range.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: She turned 18 earlier this year, so she won't be 19 until next year. She's still got plenty of time before that 31st August cutoff after turning 19.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Let me assess your eligibility based on what you've told me.

Based on our conversation:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old
- Alex is currently studying for her A-levels (approved education)
- You live in the UK with Alex
- You are the person primarily responsible for Alex and she lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- Alex is not in hospital or in care
- Alex turned 18 this year and won't turn 19 until next year, so she's well within the eligibility period (Child Benefit continues until 31 August after turning 19 for those in approved education)

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 18 and studying for her A-levels, which is approved full-time education
- Child Benefit continues for young people in education or training until **31 August after they turn 19**, so Alex is well within the eligible age range
- You live in the UK with Alex and are the person primarily responsible for her
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

**Important to remember:**
- Your Child Benefit for Alex will continue as long as she remains in approved education or training, but it will stop on **31 August after she turns 19** (even if she's still studying)
- If Alex leaves education before then, you should inform the Child Benefit Office

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you have any other questions, please let me know!

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) She is 18 years old, which is under 20; (2) She is in approved full-time education studying for A-levels; (3) She has not yet reached the age limit - Child Benefit continues for young people in approved education or training until 31 August after they turn 19, and Alex only turned 18 this year; (4) She lives with the claimant who is primarily responsible for her; (5) She is not in hospital or care; (6) The claimant lives in the UK with Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their daughter Alex. Alex meets all the criteria as she is under 20, in approved education (A-levels), hasn't reached the 31 August cutoff after turning 19, lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is the person primarily responsible for her with no one else claiming.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) She is 18 years old, which is under 20; (2) She is in approved full-time education studying for A-levels; (3) She has not yet reached the age limit - Child Benefit continues for young people in approved education or training until 31 August after they turn 19, and Alex only turned 18 this year; (4) She lives with the claimant who is primarily responsible for her; (5) She is not in hospital or care; (6) The claimant lives in the UK with Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their daughter Alex. Alex meets all the criteria as she is under 20, in approved education (A-levels), hasn't reached the 31 August cutoff after turning 19, lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is the person primarily responsible for her with no one else claiming.
